steps.title: Locate the structure around the blank
steps.content: The main clause already has a finite verb phrase ('re-created and then reprogrammed... the genetic scissors of a species of DNA-cleaving bacteria'). After the blank comes the noun phrase 'a tool that is revolutionizing the field of gene technology.' The blank must connect the action to its result/purpose.

steps.title: Apply the conventions of Standard English
steps.content: An infinitive of purpose ('to forge') cleanly links the reprogramming to its goal: they reprogrammed the scissors in order to forge a tool. This is a complete, idiomatic sentence.

steps.title: Eliminate by rule violated
steps.content:
• Choice A — to forge: infinitive of purpose correctly attaches the resulting tool to the action of reprogramming. ✓
  • Choice B — forging: a participial phrase here would need a comma before it to function as a result modifier; without the comma, it creates a run-on/unclear modification. ✗
  • Choice C — forged: a past participle without comma punctuation tries to function as a third main verb, breaking parallelism with 're-created and... reprogrammed' and creating a comma-splice/structure error. ✗
  • Choice D — and forging: mixes a participle with finite past-tense verbs joined by 'and,' violating parallelism (should be 'and forged' to parallel 're-created and reprogrammed,' but even that is wrong contextually). ✗
A is correct.
